claims.evidence_text: If drought occurs during water sensitive developmental stages (cf. Figure 3), such as germination, tillering, ﬂowering, or grain ﬁlling (Yu et al., 2018; Senapati et al., 2021; Xu et al., 2022), growth and subsequently yield can be signiﬁcantly impacted
claims.claim_summary: Wheat is most sensitive to drought at germination, tillering, flowering, and grain filling stages.

claims.evidence_text: Evaluation of past yield data and simulation model predictions point to a high risk for wheat production under climatic conditions with hot temperatures (>25°C; LüttgerandFeike,2018;Figure1B) and drought occurring at a sensitive developmental stage
claims.claim_summary: Hot temperatures above 25°C pose a high risk to wheat production.

claims.evidence_text: Reduction of yield through an increase of heat degree hours ... Heat degree hours > 27°C [h] R2=0.38, p < 0.001 2040: 515 h
claims.claim_summary: Accumulated heat degree hours above 27°C are correlated with reduced wheat yield.

claims.evidence_text: High temperature and insufﬁcient water supply lead to shorter grain ﬁlling periods and thus a low average grain weight and even shrivelled grains
claims.claim_summary: High temperature during grain filling shortens the grain-fill period and reduces grain weight in wheat.

claims.evidence_text: The main developmental stages of winter wheat from germination to ripening are depicted... Germination 0-9; Leaf development 10-19; Tillering 20-29; Stem elongation 30-39; Booting & heading 40-59; Anthesis 60-69; Grain filling & ripening 70-99
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat development follows the BBCH scale: germination 0-9, leaf development 10-19, tillering 20-29, stem elongation 30-39, booting/heading 40-59, anthesis 60-69, grain filling/ripening 70-99.
claims.table_label: Figure 3 BBCH classification of winter wheat developmental stages

claims.evidence_text: During the tiller differentiation process (BBCH 20, tillering stage, cf. Figure 3), the maximum number of tillers is established. Transitioning from BBCH 20 to BBCH 30 (stem elongation stage, cf. Figure 3), the number of established tillers is reduced to productive, spike-bearing tillers.
claims.claim_summary: Maximum wheat tiller number is established at BBCH 20 (tillering); reduction to productive tillers occurs from BBCH 20 to BBCH 30.

claims.evidence_text: The developmental stages from heading until anthesis are especially sensitive to drought stress González-Navarro et al. (2015). If drought stress is too severe, shedding of fertilised ﬂorets can occur after anthesis.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat developmental stages from heading to anthesis (BBCH 50-69) are especially sensitive to drought stress.

claims.evidence_text: In general, the longer the grain ﬁlling period during the stages of grain development and ripening (BBCH 70 and 80), the higher the average grain weight is
claims.claim_summary: A longer grain-filling period (BBCH 70-80) results in higher average wheat grain weight.

claims.evidence_text: drought stress during stem elongation can lead to yield reduction up to 71.52% (Ding et al., 2018)
claims.claim_summary: Drought stress during stem elongation can reduce wheat yield by up to 71.52%.

claims.evidence_text: the co-occurrence of heat and drought is expected to have the most signiﬁcant impact on wheat yield, with a predicted global reduction of 3.9% (Heino et al., 2023)
claims.claim_summary: Co-occurring heat and drought is predicted to reduce global wheat yield by 3.9%.

claims.evidence_text: High temperature and insufﬁcient water supply lead to... a shorter duration for the translocation of assimilates to the grain and thus lowers the harvest index of wheat
claims.claim_summary: Heat and water stress during grain fill lower the harvest index of wheat by shortening assimilate translocation to the grain.
